title: Portuguese Chicken Paella
description: A richly flavored Portuguese dish featuring paella rice and chicken, perfect for a sick day, with a peppery and flaky texture, and a sweet and sticky glaze, made with raw friendly ingredients.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large paella pan or skillet over medium high heat.
Add the chicken to the pan and cook until browned on all sides and cooked through.
Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pan and cook until softened.
Add the minced garlic to the pan and cook for 1 minute.
Add the paella rice to the pan and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper to the pan and stir to combine.
Add the chicken broth and lemon juice to the pan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20-25 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
Return the chicken to the pan and stir to combine with the rice and sauce.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the chicken is heated through.
